title: NordVPN vs Surfshark vs Proton VPN 2026: Which VPN Actually Protects You?
description: In-depth comparison of NordVPN, Surfshark, and Proton VPN in 2026. Real pricing, speed tests, privacy audits, and streaming results to find your best match.
content:
Affiliate Disclosure: This article contains affiliate links. If you purchase through our links, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. This does not influence our rankings or recommendations.

NordVPN vs Surfshark vs Proton VPN 2026: Which VPN Actually Protects You?

Last Updated: March 2026

Three VPNs. Three very different philosophies. NordVPN throws money at speed and infrastructure. Surfshark undercuts everyone on price while offering unlimited devices. Proton VPN bets everything on transparency and open-source code. Each one claims to be the best choice for your privacy in 2026.

But claims are cheap. This comparison cuts through the marketing to answer the question that actually matters: which VPN deserves your money based on real-world performance, verified privacy credentials, and honest value for what you pay?

After cross-referencing independent speed tests from CyberInsider, Tom's Guide, and TechRadar, along with published audit reports and streaming access tests, here is how these three stack up in March 2026.

Quick Verdict: Who Wins?

CategoryWinnerWhy
Best OverallNordVPNFastest speeds + most complete feature set
Best ValueSurfshark$2.19/mo + unlimited devices
Best PrivacyProton VPNSwiss jurisdiction + fully open-source + free tier
Best for StreamingNordVPNUnblocks 15+ Netflix libraries consistently
Best for TorrentingSurfsharkP2P on all servers + unlimited bandwidth
Best for FamiliesSurfsharkUnlimited simultaneous connections
Best for Journalists/ActivistsProton VPNSecure Core + Swiss legal protection
Best Free OptionProton VPNOnly reputable VPN with a genuinely usable free tier

Bottom Line: NordVPN wins the overall comparison for users who want the fastest, most reliable VPN experience and are willing to pay a modest premium for it. Surfshark is the smarter choice if budget matters and you have many devices to protect. Proton VPN is the right pick if verifiable privacy and open-source transparency outweigh everything else. There is no wrong choice among these three -- the wrong choice is using no VPN at all.

Speed Comparison: Real Numbers, Not Marketing Hype

Speed is the first thing you notice with a VPN. A slow VPN means buffering streams, laggy video calls, and frustration. Here is how these three performed in independent lab tests on 1 Gbps connections, measured across multiple server locations in early 2026.

Download Speed Results (Mbps on 1 Gbps connection)

Server LocationNordVPNSurfsharkProton VPN
New York, US825 Mbps637 Mbps580 Mbps
Los Angeles, US868 Mbps653 Mbps545 Mbps
London, UK658 Mbps556 Mbps490 Mbps
Seattle, US903 Mbps680 Mbps560 Mbps
Avg Speed Retention~81%~63%~55%

Analysis: NordVPN is clearly the fastest of the three, retaining roughly 81% of base connection speed across all tested locations. Its proprietary NordLynx protocol (built on WireGuard) consistently delivers the lowest latency and highest throughput. Surfshark holds a comfortable middle ground -- fast enough for 4K streaming and large downloads without noticeable lag. Proton VPN is the slowest, though "slow" is relative here. Even its lowest result (490 Mbps in London) is more than 19 times what you need for 4K streaming (25 Mbps).

For everyday browsing, streaming, and even gaming, all three are fast enough. The speed difference only becomes meaningful if you regularly transfer large files or have a gigabit connection and want to preserve maximum throughput.

Speed Winner: NordVPN -- by a significant margin.

Privacy & Security: Where Your Data Actually Goes

A VPN that logs your activity is worse than no VPN at all -- it creates a single point of surveillance. Here is how each provider handles the question of trust.

NordVPN Privacy

  • Jurisdiction: Panama -- no mandatory data retention laws, outside Five Eyes/Nine Eyes/Fourteen Eyes surveillance alliances
  • Logging Policy: Strict no-logs, verified by five independent audits (twice by PricewaterhouseCoopers, three times by Deloitte)
  • Server Architecture: RAM-only servers (diskless) -- physically incapable of storing persistent data. All data is wiped on every reboot
  • Encryption: AES-256-GCM with post-quantum encryption support (future-proofed against quantum computing threats)
  • Extra Security: Double VPN (routes through two servers), Onion Over VPN (routes through Tor network), Threat Protection Pro (DNS-level ad/malware blocking)

Surfshark Privacy

  • Jurisdiction: Netherlands -- within the EU and subject to GDPR, which some privacy advocates view as a concern given EU data-sharing frameworks. However, Dutch law does not mandate VPN logging
  • Logging Policy: No-logs policy independently audited twice by Deloitte (2023 and June 2025). Additional security audits by Cure53 (browser extensions) and SecuRing (full security assessment, April 2025)
  • Server Architecture: RAM-only servers across the entire 3,200+ network -- same data-wiping protection as NordVPN
  • Encryption: AES-256-GCM via WireGuard and OpenVPN
  • Extra Security: MultiHop (double VPN), Camouflage Mode (obfuscation), CleanWeb 2.0, Alternative ID (generates disposable online identities)

Proton VPN Privacy

  • Jurisdiction: Switzerland -- arguably the strongest privacy jurisdiction in the world. Outside all surveillance alliances. Swiss law provides exceptional legal protection for user data
  • Logging Policy: Strict no-logs, audited by Securitum with published public reports. Proton emphasizes repeated audits and full transparency
  • Open Source: All apps on every platform are fully open-source and available on GitHub. Anyone can inspect the code. This is the single biggest differentiator -- you do not have to trust Proton's claims because you can verify the code yourself
  • Server Architecture: Full-disk encryption on all servers. Secure Core servers are housed in hardened data centers in Switzerland, Iceland, and Sweden with biometric access controls
  • Encryption: AES-256 + ChaCha20 via WireGuard, with Stealth protocol for bypassing censorship
  • Extra Security: Secure Core (routes through privacy-friendly countries before exit), NetShield (ad/tracker/malware blocking), Tor over VPN, built by the same CERN scientists who created Proton Mail

Privacy Comparison Table

Privacy FactorNordVPNSurfsharkProton VPN
JurisdictionPanama (excellent)Netherlands (good)Switzerland (excellent)
Independent Audits5 audits (PwC + Deloitte)4 audits (Deloitte + Cure53 + SecuRing)Multiple (Securitum, published)
Open SourceNoNoYes (all platforms)
RAM-Only ServersYesYesFull-disk encryption
Warrant CanaryNoYesYes (via transparency reports)
Post-Quantum EncryptionYesNoNo

Privacy Winner: Proton VPN -- Swiss jurisdiction plus fully open-source code is an unbeatable combination. NordVPN's five audits and Panama base make it a very close second. Surfshark's Netherlands jurisdiction is the weakest link among the three, though its audit record is solid.

Streaming: Which VPN Actually Unblocks Netflix?

Streaming services spend millions blocking VPNs. Not all VPNs fight back equally. Here is who wins the arms race in March 2026.

Analysis: Surfshark technically unblocks the most Netflix regional libraries (30+), which is impressive for a budget VPN. NordVPN offers the most consistent unblocking -- when you connect to a NordVPN streaming server, it works. Every time. With minimal fuss. Proton VPN is the weakest for streaming. It handles major platforms on paid plans but has gaps with Disney+ and Amazon Prime Video, and the free tier does not unblock any streaming services at all.

Streaming Winner: NordVPN -- most reliable across all platforms. Surfshark gets an honorable mention for sheer library count.

Torrenting & P2P: Which VPN Keeps You Safe?

All three VPNs allow P2P file sharing, but the implementations differ.

  • NordVPN: Dedicated P2P servers optimized for torrenting. SOCKS5 proxy included for torrent clients. No bandwidth throttling. Kill switch prevents IP leaks if connection drops
  • Surfshark: P2P allowed on all 3,200+ servers -- no need to manually select a specific server category. Unlimited bandwidth and unlimited devices means you can torrent on multiple machines simultaneously
  • Proton VPN: P2P supported on paid plans across most servers. Port forwarding available (a feature many torrenters want that NordVPN and Surfshark lack). Free tier does not support P2P

Torrenting Winner: Surfshark -- P2P on every server plus unlimited devices at the lowest price. Proton VPN's port forwarding is a nice bonus for power users.

Price Analysis: Surfshark is the cheapest by a meaningful margin. At $2.19/month for a 2-year plan with unlimited devices, no other top-tier VPN comes close. If you have a family of four with 3 devices each, Surfshark covers all 12 devices for $2.19/month. NordVPN and Proton VPN would cover 10 devices for $3.39/month and $4.49/month respectively.

Proton VPN is the most expensive paid option, but it is also the only one with a genuinely usable free tier. If you just need basic privacy for browsing on a single device, Proton VPN Free costs nothing and does not bombard you with ads or sell your data.

Value Winner: Surfshark -- $2.19/mo with unlimited devices is hard to argue with. Proton VPN wins if you want to pay nothing.

NordVPN Deep Dive

What Makes NordVPN Stand Out in 2026

NordVPN has evolved from a simple VPN into a comprehensive cybersecurity platform. The 2026 version includes Threat Protection Pro (a DNS-level malware and ad blocker that works even when the VPN is disconnected), Dark Web Monitor (alerts you if your credentials appear in data breaches), and post-quantum encryption that future-proofs your connections against quantum computing attacks.

The NordLynx protocol remains the fastest consumer VPN protocol available. Built on WireGuard with a proprietary double NAT system, it delivers speeds that competing VPNs simply cannot match. In testing across four major server locations, NordVPN never dropped below 658 Mbps on a gigabit connection.

The server network spans 7,000+ servers across 118 countries (expanding to 9,000+ on higher-tier plans), with specialty servers for Double VPN, Onion Over VPN, P2P, and obfuscated connections. This breadth of infrastructure means less server congestion and more options for finding a fast, nearby connection.

NordVPN Pros

  • Fastest speeds of any consumer VPN in 2026 (81% speed retention)
  • Five independent no-logs audits -- the most verified privacy claims in the industry
  • Post-quantum encryption support (future-proofing)
  • Unblocks 15+ Netflix libraries and every major streaming platform reliably
  • Threat Protection Pro works as a standalone security tool even with VPN off
  • RAM-only servers make data retention physically impossible

NordVPN Cons

  • More expensive than Surfshark ($3.39 vs $2.19/mo on 2-year plans)
  • 10-device limit -- not ideal for large households (Surfshark offers unlimited)
  • Not open-source -- you must trust audits rather than verify code yourself
  • Monthly price ($12.99) is steep if you do not want a long-term commitment
  • Occasional connection drops on popular US servers during peak hours

Surfshark Deep Dive

What Makes Surfshark Stand Out in 2026

Surfshark's defining feature is unlimited simultaneous connections. Every other major VPN caps you at 5-10 devices. Surfshark puts no limit. One subscription covers your phone, laptop, tablet, smart TV, router, gaming console, your partner's devices, your kids' devices -- everything. For families or anyone with a lot of hardware, this alone justifies the choice.

The 2026 version of Surfshark includes several features that punch well above its price point. Alternative ID generates disposable personal information (name, email, address) for sign-ups, reducing your exposure to data breaches. CleanWeb 2.0 blocks ads, trackers, cookie pop-ups, and known malware domains across all connected devices.

All 3,200+ servers now support 10 Gbps throughput, a tenfold increase over the standard 1 Gbps infrastructure that many budget VPNs still use. Combined with WireGuard as the default protocol, this infrastructure investment has substantially closed the speed gap with NordVPN compared to previous years.

Surfshark Pros

  • Unlimited simultaneous device connections -- no competitor matches this at this price
  • Cheapest top-tier VPN at $2.19/month (2-year Starter plan)
  • Unblocks 30+ Netflix regional libraries -- more than NordVPN or Proton VPN
  • Alternative ID feature is unique and genuinely useful for privacy-conscious users
  • 7-day free trial on mobile with no credit card required
  • All servers upgraded to 10 Gbps infrastructure in 2026
  • P2P allowed on every server (no need to find specialized P2P servers)

Surfshark Cons

  • Netherlands jurisdiction is less privacy-friendly than Panama or Switzerland
  • Slower than NordVPN (63% vs 81% speed retention) -- noticeable on fast connections
  • Smaller server network (3,200 vs NordVPN's 7,000+) means more congestion at peak times
  • Owned by Nord Security (same parent company as NordVPN since 2022) -- independence concerns
  • Customer support response times lag behind NordVPN by several minutes
  • Not open-source

Proton VPN Deep Dive

What Makes Proton VPN Stand Out in 2026

Proton VPN was built by the same team of scientists and engineers from CERN and MIT who created Proton Mail, the world's most popular encrypted email service. This pedigree matters because it means privacy is not a marketing angle -- it is the company's founding mission.

The single most important differentiator: Proton VPN is fully open-source. Every app on every platform -- Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS -- has its source code published on GitHub. Independent security researchers can (and do) inspect, test, and verify the code. When Proton says "no logs," you do not have to take their word for it. You can read the code.

Switzerland provides what is arguably the strongest legal framework for privacy in the world. Swiss courts have historically sided with individual privacy rights, and the country sits outside all major surveillance alliances. Proton VPN's Secure Core architecture routes sensitive traffic through hardened servers in Switzerland, Iceland, and Sweden before reaching the exit server, providing protection even in the unlikely event that an exit server is compromised.

The free tier is genuinely usable. Unlike "free" VPNs that cap you at 500MB/month or throttle speeds to unusable levels, Proton VPN Free offers unlimited data with no artificial speed caps, access to servers in five countries, and absolutely no ads. The catch: one device only, five country options, and no streaming unblocking. For basic browsing privacy, it is the only free VPN worth recommending.

Proton VPN Pros

  • 100% open-source on all platforms -- verifiable privacy, not just promised privacy
  • Swiss jurisdiction -- the gold standard for legal privacy protection
  • Genuinely usable free tier with unlimited data and no ads
  • Secure Core servers in hardened data centers with biometric access controls
  • 9,200+ servers across 112 countries -- massive network rivaling NordVPN
  • Built by CERN/MIT scientists with a proven track record (Proton Mail)
  • Port forwarding available for torrenting power users
  • Stealth protocol for bypassing VPN blocks in censored countries

Proton VPN Cons

  • Slowest of the three (55% speed retention vs 81% for NordVPN)
  • Most expensive paid option ($4.49/mo vs $2.19 for Surfshark)
  • Streaming unblocking is inconsistent -- Disney+ and Amazon Prime Video fail in some regions
  • Free tier limited to 1 device, 5 countries, and no streaming
  • No dedicated IP option
  • Interface is less polished than NordVPN or Surfshark

Feature-by-Feature Breakdown

Kill Switch

All three include kill switches, but Proton VPN's is the most aggressive -- it can be set to "always on" mode, which blocks all internet traffic unless the VPN is connected, even after a reboot. NordVPN and Surfshark offer app-level and system-level kill switches that activate only when an active VPN connection drops.

Split Tunneling

All three support split tunneling (routing some apps through VPN and others through your regular connection). Surfshark's implementation is the most intuitive, letting you configure it with a few clicks. NordVPN offers this on Windows, Android, and macOS. Proton VPN supports it on Windows, Android, and Linux.

Ad and Malware Blocking

NordVPN's Threat Protection Pro is the most comprehensive, functioning as a lightweight antivirus that blocks malicious files at the download level -- even when VPN is disconnected. Surfshark's CleanWeb 2.0 and Proton VPN's NetShield both block ads, trackers, and known malware domains at the DNS level, which is effective but less comprehensive than NordVPN's approach.

Multi-Hop (Double VPN)

All three offer multi-hop routing for enhanced security. NordVPN calls it Double VPN, Surfshark calls it MultiHop, and Proton VPN offers Secure Core (which routes through privacy-friendly countries specifically). Proton VPN's approach is the most thoughtful because the intermediate servers are in jurisdictions chosen specifically for their privacy laws.

Obfuscation (Bypassing VPN Blocks)

For users in countries that actively block VPN traffic (China, Iran, Russia), obfuscation is essential. NordVPN offers obfuscated servers. Surfshark has Camouflage Mode. Proton VPN's Stealth protocol is purpose-built for this use case and is generally considered the most effective of the three for bypassing deep packet inspection.

Who Should Choose Which VPN

Choose NordVPN If You...

  • Want the fastest possible VPN speeds for streaming, gaming, or large downloads
  • Need reliable access to Netflix, Disney+, Hulu, BBC iPlayer, and other streaming platforms
  • Want the most rigorously audited privacy credentials (five independent audits)
  • Value Threat Protection Pro as a standalone security feature
  • Are willing to pay a moderate premium for the best overall experience
  • Have 10 or fewer devices to protect

Get NordVPN -- starting at $3.39/month

Choose Surfshark If You...

  • Have a large household with many devices and need unlimited connections
  • Want the cheapest possible top-tier VPN without sacrificing core quality
  • Care about accessing the widest range of Netflix regional libraries (30+)
  • Want a VPN that does not require any technical knowledge to configure
  • Need P2P support on every server without hunting for specialty servers
  • Are a student or budget-conscious user

Get Surfshark -- starting at $2.19/month

Choose Proton VPN If You...

  • Consider privacy your absolute top priority and want to verify claims yourself
  • Need a genuinely usable free VPN with no data caps or ads
  • Are a journalist, activist, researcher, or anyone handling sensitive information
  • Want a VPN built by a company whose entire business model is privacy (Proton Mail, Proton Drive)
  • Prefer Swiss legal jurisdiction over Panama or the Netherlands
  • Use Linux as your primary operating system (Proton VPN has excellent Linux support)
  • Need port forwarding for torrenting

Get Proton VPN -- free tier available, paid from $4.49/month

Mobile App Experience

Most VPN usage happens on phones. A VPN that works flawlessly on desktop but stutters on mobile is not worth recommending. Here is how the mobile apps compare in March 2026.

NordVPN Mobile

NordVPN's mobile apps (iOS and Android) are polished and fast. The interface mirrors the desktop experience with a clean map view showing server locations. One-tap Quick Connect selects the optimal server automatically. Battery impact is minimal thanks to NordLynx's efficiency -- in typical use, expect 3-5% additional battery drain over a full day. The app includes all core features: kill switch, split tunneling (Android), Threat Protection, and Dark Web Monitor. App Store rating: 4.7/5 on iOS, 4.5/5 on Google Play.

Surfshark Mobile

Surfshark's mobile app is arguably the most beginner-friendly of the three. Large connect buttons, clear server lists, and a straightforward settings menu make it accessible even for non-technical users. CleanWeb works on mobile to block in-app ads (Android) and Safari ads (iOS). The app includes a GPS spoofing feature on Android that overrides your phone's GPS location -- useful for apps that check GPS rather than IP address. App Store rating: 4.6/5 on iOS, 4.3/5 on Google Play.

Proton VPN Mobile

Proton VPN's mobile app prioritizes function over flash. The design is clean but utilitarian compared to NordVPN and Surfshark. The standout feature is the always-on VPN mode, which ensures your phone never connects to the internet without VPN protection -- even after a restart. The free tier works on mobile with no ads or nag screens, making it the best free mobile VPN available. Secure Core and NetShield are available on mobile for paid users. App Store rating: 4.6/5 on iOS, 4.3/5 on Google Play.

Mobile Winner: NordVPN

NordVPN's combination of speed, polish, and feature completeness on mobile gives it the edge. Surfshark's GPS spoofing is a unique advantage for Android users. Proton VPN's free mobile app is the best option if you refuse to pay.

Customer Support Comparison

When your VPN stops working at 11 PM on a Sunday night and you need to catch a live stream, customer support quality matters more than any spec sheet.

NordVPN's live chat is the fastest in the industry -- responses typically arrive within 30 seconds, and agents can troubleshoot technical issues in real-time. Surfshark's live chat is responsive but slightly slower during peak hours. Proton VPN relies primarily on email support, which means waiting hours or sometimes a day for complex issues. Proton compensates with an active Reddit community and detailed documentation, but for users who need immediate help, this is a notable weakness.

Support Winner: NordVPN -- fastest live chat response times and the most comprehensive support infrastructure.

Common VPN Myths Debunked

Myth: A VPN Makes You Completely Anonymous

No VPN provides total anonymity. Your VPN provider can still theoretically see your traffic (which is why no-logs audits matter). Your browser fingerprint, cookies, and login behavior can identify you regardless of VPN usage. A VPN is one layer of privacy -- not a magic cloak.

Myth: Free VPNs Are Just As Good

Most free VPNs monetize by selling your data, injecting ads, or both. A 2024 study by top10vpn.com found that 72% of free VPN apps on Android contained at least one tracker. Proton VPN is the sole exception among major providers -- its free tier is funded by paid subscribers, not by exploiting free users.

Myth: More Servers Always Means Better Performance

Server count matters less than server quality and geographic placement. Proton VPN has 9,200+ servers but is slower than NordVPN with 7,000+. What matters is server infrastructure (10 Gbps vs 1 Gbps), protocol efficiency, and network management. NordVPN's smaller-but-optimized network outperforms larger competitors.

Myth: VPNs Are Only for Illegal Activity

VPN usage is mainstream. Over 1.6 billion people worldwide use VPNs for entirely legitimate purposes: protecting public WiFi connections, preventing ISP tracking, accessing content while traveling, securing remote work, and exercising basic digital privacy rights. Your ISP in the US can legally sell your browsing data -- a VPN prevents that.

After comparing pricing, speed, privacy, streaming, and real-world usability, here is the definitive ranking for March 2026:

  • Speed and reliability? NordVPN.

If you are still undecided, start with Proton VPN's free tier to experience what a VPN does. If you decide you want faster speeds and streaming access, upgrade to NordVPN or Surfshark. All three offer 30-day money-back guarantees, so you can try any of them risk-free.

The worst VPN choice in 2026 is no VPN at all. With ISPs legally selling browsing data, public WiFi attacks at an all-time high, and data breaches exposing billions of records annually, protecting your connection is not optional -- it is basic digital hygiene.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is NordVPN better than Surfshark in 2026?

NordVPN is better for speed and streaming reliability. In independent tests, NordVPN retains 81% of base connection speed compared to Surfshark's 63%. However, Surfshark is better for value ($2.19 vs $3.39/month) and offers unlimited device connections. If speed is your priority, NordVPN wins. If budget and device count matter more, Surfshark wins.

Is Proton VPN really free?

Yes. Proton VPN offers a genuinely free tier with unlimited data, no speed caps, and no advertisements. The free plan includes servers in 5 countries and supports 1 device. It does not include streaming unblocking or P2P support. Unlike most free VPNs, Proton does not sell user data -- the free tier is subsidized by paying customers.

Can NordVPN, Surfshark, or Proton VPN unblock Netflix?

All three can unblock Netflix on paid plans. NordVPN reliably accesses 15+ Netflix regional libraries and is the most consistent performer. Surfshark accesses 30+ libraries but occasional failures occur. Proton VPN accesses approximately 10 libraries on paid plans. Proton VPN's free tier does not unblock any streaming services.

Which VPN is safest for torrenting?

All three are safe for torrenting with strong encryption and no-logs policies. Surfshark allows P2P on all servers with unlimited bandwidth. NordVPN offers dedicated P2P servers. Proton VPN supports P2P on paid plans and offers port forwarding, which can improve download speeds. All three include kill switches to prevent IP exposure if the VPN connection drops.

Does Surfshark really offer unlimited devices?

Yes. Surfshark places no limit on the number of devices you can connect simultaneously with a single subscription. This includes phones, laptops, tablets, smart TVs, routers, gaming consoles, and any other device that supports a VPN connection. NordVPN and Proton VPN both cap at 10 simultaneous connections.

Which VPN has the best privacy jurisdiction?

Proton VPN (Switzerland) and NordVPN (Panama) are both in excellent privacy jurisdictions outside surveillance alliances. Switzerland benefits from some of the strongest privacy laws in the world. Panama has no mandatory data retention laws. Surfshark's Netherlands base is within the EU, which provides GDPR protection but is part of the Nine Eyes surveillance alliance -- a concern for some privacy advocates.

Are these VPNs safe for public WiFi?

Yes. All three encrypt your traffic with AES-256 or equivalent encryption, making it unreadable to anyone on the same public WiFi network. This protects against man-in-the-middle attacks, packet sniffing, and evil twin hotspots -- the most common public WiFi threats. Any of these three VPNs is dramatically safer than using public WiFi unprotected.

Should I get NordVPN Basic or Plus plan?

For most users, the Basic plan ($3.39/month) provides everything you need -- full VPN access, NordLynx protocol, and basic threat protection. The Plus plan ($4.39/month) adds Threat Protection Pro (enhanced malware blocking) and NordPass password manager. If you do not already use a password manager, the Plus plan offers meaningful added value for just $1 more per month. If you already use a dedicated password manager like 1Password or Bitwarden, the Basic plan is sufficient.

Prices verified March 2026. Speed test data sourced from CyberInsider, Tom's Guide, and TechRadar. Privacy audit information from published reports by Deloitte, PwC, Securitum, Cure53, and SecuRing. Streaming data from independent testing by CyberInsider, PCWorld, and Security.org.

schema.headline: NordVPN vs Surfshark vs Proton VPN 2026: Which VPN Actually Protects You?

mainEntity.name: Is NordVPN better than Surfshark in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: NordVPN is better for speed and streaming reliability, retaining 81% of base connection speed compared to Surfshark's 63%. However, Surfshark is better for value ($2.19 vs $3.39/month) and offers unlimited device connections. If speed is your priority, NordVPN wins. If budget and device count matter more, Surfshark wins.

Yes. Proton VPN offers a genuinely free tier with unlimited data, no speed caps, and no advertisements. The free plan includes servers in 5 countries and supports 1 device. Unlike most free VPNs, Proton does not sell user data — the free tier is subsidized by paying customers.

mainEntity.name: Can NordVPN, Surfshark, or Proton VPN unblock Netflix?

acceptedAnswer.text: All three can unblock Netflix on paid plans. NordVPN reliably accesses 15+ Netflix regional libraries. Surfshark accesses 30+ libraries. Proton VPN accesses approximately 10 libraries on paid plans. Proton VPN's free tier does not unblock any streaming services.

mainEntity.name: Which VPN is safest for torrenting?

acceptedAnswer.text: All three are safe for torrenting with strong encryption and no-logs policies. Surfshark allows P2P on all servers with unlimited bandwidth. NordVPN offers dedicated P2P servers. Proton VPN supports P2P on paid plans and offers port forwarding, which can improve download speeds.

mainEntity.name: Does Surfshark really offer unlimited devices?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Surfshark places no limit on the number of devices you can connect simultaneously with a single subscription. NordVPN and Proton VPN both cap at 10 simultaneous connections.

mainEntity.name: Which VPN has the best privacy jurisdiction?

acceptedAnswer.text: Proton VPN (Switzerland) and NordVPN (Panama) are both in excellent privacy jurisdictions outside surveillance alliances. Surfshark's Netherlands base is within the EU and part of the Nine Eyes surveillance alliance, which is a concern for some privacy advocates.

mainEntity.name: Are these VPNs safe for public WiFi?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. All three encrypt your traffic with AES-256 or equivalent encryption, making it unreadable to anyone on the same public WiFi network. Any of these three VPNs is dramatically safer than using public WiFi unprotected.

If you do not already use a password manager, the Plus plan offers meaningful added value for just $1 more per month.
